title: chionochloa conspicua subsp. conspicua
intro: Chionochloa conspicua subsp. conspicua, also known as the New Zealand hair grass, is a species of tussock-forming grass native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Chionochloa conspicua subsp. conspicua has been reported to have potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
